attacking_style: Attacks on punchy, explosive climbs (10-12% gradients) rather than sustained long climbs. Prefers bridging to moves or following accelerations in final 30-40km rather than initiating solo attacks from distance.
team_tactics: Team leadership often unclear with shared responsibilities (Kwiatkowski at Amstel). Used as high-altitude domestique for GC riders when not leading. Limited evidence of coordinated lead-out or tempo control from teammates.
terrain_preference: Explosive punchy climbs (10-12%) and technical circuits suit best. Struggles on sustained pure climbing efforts and longer Ardennes-style drags. Time trial capable (beat Ganna in Tour debut). Excels on technical descents and positioning battles.
vulnerability: Distanced on sustained climbs and longer drags (dropped Côte de Cherave, distanced Amstel climbs, only closed gaps on flatter sections). Illness disrupts form significantly (2022 Fleche). Crash-prone with multiple incidents across seasons.
key_patterns: Crash incidents frequent but recovery capacity exceptional (finished 6th Fleche after crash relay, 2023)
Elite positioning instincts: bridges to key moves and follows accelerations rather than solo attacks (4 instances)
Stronger on explosive punchy gradients 10-12% than sustained climbing (Fleche dropped, Tour stage podiums)
Time trial ability exceeds typical classics specialist profile (beat Ganna Tour debut TT)
Form fragility: illness and crashes disrupt preparation, affecting performance windows (2022 Fleche, 2023 Tirreno crash)
Extending climbing range for stage races but still limited on pure climbing tests (LBL 2nd notable)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed range extension (LBL 2nd) but also form inconsistency (Tirreno crash/struggles). Transition from classics focus to GC domestique role by Tour suggests tactical repositioning rather than pure form trajectory.
